revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23989927232|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:備中国総社宮]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Bitchū Kokusoja-gū | location = 2-18-1 Sōja, Sōja City, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|qid=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}} | type = 34.676961 | longitude = 133.753878 | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} | map = Japan Okayama | type = {{ill|Bitchū Province|en|Bitchū Province|qid=Q1047514|lt=Bitchū Province|1=Bitchū Province}} {{ill|Sōja|en|Sōja|qid=Q873536|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja}}
Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | festivals = Third Saturday and Sunday of October (Main festival) | Rituals = {{nihongo|Wakuguri|輪くぐり|qq=}}
{{nihongo|Chikara-ishi Competition|力石総社|qq=}} | image = File:Soja in Soja.jpg | prefecture= {{ill|Soja|qid=Q873536|1=Sōja}} | coordinates= {{Coord|34.676961|N|133.753878|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Bitchū Kokusoja-gū''' ({{lang|ja|備中国総社宮|qq=}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Sōja, Okayama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja, Okayama|qq=}}. It served as the {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|qid=Q1107129|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja shrine}} (regional shrine) of {{ill|Bitchū Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bitchū Province|qq=}} and is officially known as "Sōja" (總社). It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine|Prefectural Shrine]] under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代Rank制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=10056 總社] (Okayama Shrine Association). == Overview == [[File:總社 (備中国) 鳥居.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii Gate]] The shrine is located in the southern part of {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|qid=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}, in the center of Sōja City. The city and station names derive from this shrine. Historically, provincial governors ({{ill|Kokushi (official)|en|Kokushi (official)|qid=Q1351234|lt=Kokushi|1=Kokushi (official)}}) visited all shrines in their jurisdiction from the ichinomiya downward. To streamline this practice, a {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|qid=Q1107129|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja shrine}} was established near the provincial capital ({{ill|Kokufu|en|Kokufu|qid=Q1629088|lt=Kokufu|1=Kokufu}}), where deities from multiple shrines were enshrined and worshipped together. This shrine served as the sōja of Bitchū Province. The shrine's garden features a traditional {{nihongo|Mishima-style|三島式|qq=}} layout, reflecting ancient designs that influenced the construction of {{nihongo|Kōraku-en|後楽園|qq=}}, one of Japan's Three Great Gardens. The shrine grounds are designated as a historic site by Sōja City. == Deities == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大名持命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Ōkuninushi) * {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime-no-Mikoto|ja|須世理姫命|sv|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スセリビメ}} – Wife of Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto. ; Enshrined Deities * {{nihongo|Yasaka Shrine's Eight Deities|御鎮魂八柱神|qq=}} – [[Takamimusubi]], [[Kamimusubi]], Tamadome-Musubi, {{ill|Amatsukashitama|ja|天活玉命|1=Ame-no-Ikutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukashitama|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Ikutama|qq=}}, Ashitsukuri-Musubi, {{ill|Ōmiyame|ja|大宮売神|1=Ōmiya-no-Me|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyame|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, and Miketsu-no-Kami. * Deities from 304 shrines in Bitchū Province – Includes 18 from the {{nihongo|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|延喜式神名帳|qq=}} and 286 from other records. == History == Before the establishment of the shrine, the area was linked to Emperor {{nihongo|Nintoku|仁徳天皇|qq=}}'s consort, {{nihongo|Princess Hata|八田皇女|qq=}}, and her associated territories ({{nihongo|Nashi|名代|qq=}}).Shrine historical plaque. Later, descendants of {{ill|Miyotowake|ja|御友別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyotowake|qq=}}, brother of Empress {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Ojin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, are believed to have settled here, founding a shrine to {{nihongo|Numata Shrine|沼田神社|qq=}}, considered the predecessor of Bitchū Kokusoja.''Okayama Prefecture Place names'' (岡山県の地名), entry for Sōja. Following the {{nihongo|Taika Reform|大化改新|qq=}} in 645, the sōja system was formalized, and the shrine was established. In the {{nihongo|Muromachi period|1336–1573|qq=}}, the shrine suffered damage during conflicts but was rebuilt in 1429. However, it was burned again during the {{nihongo|Tenshō era|1573–1592|qq=}} and reconstructed in 1687. Prosperity grew with the support of local merchants, who donated elaborate {{ill|Ema (Shinto)|en|Ema (Shinto)|qid=Q687288|lt=Ema (Shinto)|1=Ema (Shinto)}} painted by prominent Kyoto artists. The surrounding area developed into a bustling {{ill|Monzen-machi|lt=Monzen-machi|lt=Monzen-machi|ja|門前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monzen-machi|qq=}} (shrine town) and {{ill|Shukuba|ja|宿場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shukuba|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宿場}} (post town), forming the foundation of modern Sōja City. In 1872, the shrine was designated as a [[Prefectural Shrine|Prefectural Shrine]]. == Shrine Grounds == The shrine houses several notable artworks, including "義家観雁図" by {{ill|Maruyama Ōkyo|en|Maruyama Ōkyo|qid=Q405444|lt=Maruyama Ōkyo|1=Maruyama Ōkyo}} and "林和靖図" by {{nihongo|Ōhara Tonshū|大原呑舟|qq=}}. File:總社 (備中国) 本殿.JPG|main hall
{{small|Rebuilt in 1979.|qq=}} File:總社 (備中国) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall (Haiden) File:總社 (備中国) 随神門.JPG|Zuijinmon Gate and Corridor
{{small|Corridors connect the shrine approach.|qq=}} File:總社 (備中国) 宮池.JPG|Garden
{{small|Inspired the design of {{nihongo|Kōraku-en|後楽園|qq=}}.|qq=}} == Auxiliary Shrines == [[File:沼田神社 (備中国總社境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Numata Shrine
{{nihongo|Engishiki|en|qq=}}-listed Numata Shrine.]] * '''Ōgami Shrine''' – Enshrines {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|qid=Q10936701|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|1=Ōmononushi}} (gentle spirit of Ōkuninushi). Main festival: third weekend of October. * '''Aragami Shrine''' – Enshrines {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} and {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}. Main festival: third weekend of October. * '''Seishōkō Shrine''' – Enshrines {{ill|Katō Kiyomasa|en|Katō Kiyomasa|qid=Q1069843|lt=Katō Kiyomasa|1=Katō Kiyomasa}}. Main festival: third weekend of October. * '''Gion Shrine''' – Enshrines {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. Main festival: {{nihongo|June 13|Lunisolar June13日|qq=}}. Brought from {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|qid=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}} in Kyoto. * '''Kompira Shrine''' – Enshrines Ōmononushi. Main festival: {{nihongo|June 9|Lunisolar June9日|qq=}}. * '''Numata Shrine (Tenmangū)''' – Enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}}. {{nihongo|Engishiki|en|qq=}}-listed Numata Shrine. * '''Warashi Shrine''' – Enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en|Kotoshironushi|qid=Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. Main festival: {{nihongo|January 9–10|1月9日・10日|qq=}}. * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' – Enshrines {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}. Main festival: {{nihongo|June 17|Lunisolar June17日|qq=}}. == Major Events == * '''Wakuguri''' – A traditional passing-through ring ritual. * '''Chikara-ishi Competition''' – Strength contest held since 1994.[https://web.archive.org/web/20190902201641/https://www.sanyonews.jp/article/932095 Strength contests at Chikara-ishi Sōja] - {{ill|Sanyo Shimbun|en|Sanyo Shimbun|qid=Q7420810|lt=Sanyō Shimbun|1=Sanyo Shimbun}} * '''324 Deities festival''' – Offers tributes to 324 deities, including 8 shrine deities, 12 auxiliary shrine deities, and 304 from across Bitchū Province. == Cultural Properties == === Sōja City Designated Cultural Property === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Sōja Shrine Site – Designated on October 24, 1994.[http://www.city.soja.okayama.jp/bunka/kanko/shitei_bunkazai/si/si_30.html Sōja Shrine Site] (Sōja City Website). == location and Access == ; '''location''' * 2-18-1 Sōja, Sōja City, Okayama Prefecture ; '''Transportation''' * '''By train:''' **JR {{ill|Kibi Line|en|Kibi Line|qid=Q844682|lt=Kibi Line|1=Kibi Line}} ([[:en:Momotarō Line|Momotarō Line]]): {{ill|Higashi-Sōja Station|en|Higashi-Sōja Station|qid=Q3661745|lt=Higashi-Sōja Station|1=Higashi-Sōja Station}} (5-minute walk) **JR {{ill|Hakubi Line|en|Hakubi Line|qid=Q844666|lt=Hakubi Line|1=Hakubi Line}} and [[Ibara Railway Line]]: {{ill|Sōja Station|en|Sōja Station|qid=Q844657|lt=Sōja Station|1=Sōja Station}} (20-minute walk) == References == {{reflist|qq=}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical plaque * ''Okayama Prefecture Place names'' (岡山県の地名), entry for Sōja * ''Engishiki Shrine Research Report,'' [[:en:Kōgakkan University|Kōgakkan University]] == See Also == * [[Sōja shrine|Sōja (shrine)]] == External Links == * [http://www.soja-soja.jp/ Official Website of Bitchū Kokusoja-gū] * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=10056 Sōja] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{Sōja shrines|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|備中国総社宮|version=101844785|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:備中国総社宮]] {{Commons category|Soja (Shrine in Soja, Okayama)}} {{wikidata link|Q11608173}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Japanese gardens in Chūgoku region]] [[Category:Bitchū Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sōja, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Sōja, Okayama]]
